title: What to know about Valeriana officinalis before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Valeriana officinalis, commonly known as valerian, is a perennial herb used to treat anxiety, insomnia, restlessness, and digestive issues. Its main medicinal uses include alleviating symptoms of menopause, ADHD, and irritable bowel syndrome. Valerian root has several health benefits, including promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving sleep quality. The herb's therapeutic properties include sedative, anxiolytic, and antispasmodic effects. Valerian root contains bioactive compounds like valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valeranone, which are often extracted for herbal preparations such as valerian tea or supplements.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the health benefits of Zingiber officinale include its ability to reduce inflammation, as demonstrated by its reduction of carrageenan-induced paw swelling in rats. Additionally, the extract exhibited antipyretic properties, as it reduced yeast-induced fever in rats, and also showed antimicrobial activity by significantly inhibiting the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. Furthermore, the extract was found to have blood glucose-lowering effects in rabbits, suggesting its potential in managing hypoglycaemia.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Valeriana officinalis are treating insomnia by reducing sleep latency through its sedative properties, primarily due to the valerenic acid and valepotriates constituents that inhibit the activity of GABA receptors, thereby promoting relaxation. Additionally, it is used to alleviate anxiety, restlessness, and nervousness by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and GABA, which are involved in regulating emotional responses. Valeriana officinalis has also been used to alleviate digestive issues, including gastrointestinal spasms, and muscle spasms, due to its antispasmodic properties, attributed to the valepotriates and isovaleran constituents. Furthermore, it has been used to alleviate menstrual cramps, hypertension, and rheumatism, as well as epilepsy, due to its antispasmodic, sedative, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are primarily attributed to the valepotriates and isovaleran constituents. The valepotriates and isovaleran constituents in Valeriana officinalis are also responsible for its ability to reduce inflammation and muscle spasms.

uses_list.description: This plant is used for insomnia due to its sedative properties, which are attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and valeranone, compounds that have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system and promote relaxation, thus helping to improve sleep quality.

The plant is used to alleviate anxiety due to its anxiolytic properties, which are thought to be caused by the interaction of valerenic acid and valeranone with the GABA receptor, a neurotransmitter system involved in regulating anxiety and stress response.

This plant is used to treat restlessness due to its sedative and calming effects, which help to reduce feelings of unease and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating restlessness and improving overall well-being.

The plant is used to treat digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, due to its antispasmodic properties, which help to relax smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of cramping, bloating, and discomfort.
This plant is used to treat muscle spasms due to its antispasmodic properties, which help to relax smooth muscle and reduce muscle tension, thereby alleviating spasms and cramping.
The plant is used to treat menstrual cramps due to its antispasmodic and sedative properties, which help to relax smooth muscle and reduce pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.

This plant is used to treat hypertension due to its vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure, thereby alleviating hypertension and improving cardiovascular health.
The plant is used to treat nervousness due to its sedative and calming effects, which help to reduce feelings of anxiety and unease, promoting relaxation and improving overall well-being.

This plant has been traditionally used to treat epilepsy due to its anticonvulsant properties, which help to reduce the frequency and severity of seizures, although its effectiveness in this regard is still being researched.

The plant is used to treat rheumatism due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with rheumatism, promoting relaxation and improving overall well-being.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Valeriana officinalis is used to treat insomnia by regulating sleep patterns, as well as anxiety through its anxiolytic properties. Additionally, muscle spasms are alleviated by the plant's antispasmodic effects, and restlessness is reduced due to its sedative action. Furthermore, Valeriana officinalis is also used to alleviate menopause symptoms, including hot flashes and night sweats, and has been studied for its potential in treating depression by modulating neurotransmitter levels and reducing cortisol production. Studies have also explored its use in managing epilepsy due to its anticonvulsant properties and has been found to be effective in reducing symptoms of restless leg syndrome. Valeriana officinalis has also been investigated for its potential in treating irritability and menopause symptoms, with research indicating its efficacy in reducing symptoms of these conditions.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Valeriana officinalis is used to treat insomnia, a condition characterized by difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep, by promoting relaxation and reducing stress hormones. Additionally, it is used to alleviate anxiety, a common condition that can manifest as excessive worry or fear. Valeriana officinalis is also employed to address digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, by reducing inflammation and improving gut motility. Furthermore, it is used to treat restlessness, which can be caused by an imbalance of yin and yang energies. Valeriana officinalis is also used to alleviate menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, hypertension, nervousness, depression, and premenstrual syndrome (PMS), among other conditions, due to its sedative and antispasmodic properties.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Valeriana officinalis is used to treat various health conditions, primarily insomnia, caused by its sedative properties. It is also used to alleviate anxiety by regulating the nervous system, reducing symptoms of digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome, and calming restlessness. Furthermore, Valeriana officinalis has been used to treat epilepsy by stabilizing the nervous system, and menstrual cramps through its analgesic properties. Additionally, it has been known to help with hypertension by reducing blood pressure, muscle spasms through its antispasmodic properties, and stress through its adaptogenic effects. Its anxiolytic and sedative properties also help with nervousness.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Valeriana officinalis is used to treat a range of health conditions, primarily insomnia, which affects the sleep-wake cycle. The roots and rhizomes of this plant are also used to alleviate anxiety, a mental health disorder characterized by excessive worry and fear. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis is employed to address digestive issues, restlessness, and rheumatism, with its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties providing relief from muscle cramps and joint pain. Furthermore, it is used to calm hysteria, a psychological condition marked by intense anxiety or excitement, and to control epilepsy, a neurological disorder that causes seizures. The plant's sedative and vasodilatory effects also make it beneficial for managing hypertension, nervousness, and menstrual cramps, a common symptom of premenstrual syndrome.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Valeriana officinalis is used to alleviate anxiety, a common condition characterized by excessive worry and fear. The plant's sedative properties also make it effective in treating insomnia, a sleep disorder that affects millions worldwide. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis is known to calm restlessness, a symptom often associated with muscle spasms, which can be painful and debilitating. Furthermore, its nervine properties help to reduce nervousness, which can manifest as digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, or exacerbate existing sleep disorders. Valeriana officinalis is also used to treat menstrual cramps, hypertension, and headaches, making it a versatile remedy in the realm of homeopathic medicine.
benefits_description: Valeriana officinalis reduces anxiety by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters like GABA and serotonin, thus promoting a sense of calmness. Valeriana officinalis promotes relaxation by increasing the production of GABA, the primary inhibitory neurotransmitter in the brain, which helps to regulate the activity of neurons. Valeriana officinalis improves sleep quality by regulating the body's internal clock and promoting a restful sleep pattern, reducing the symptoms of insomnia. Valeriana officinalis enhances calmness by acting on the limbic system, which is responsible for emotional responses, and decreasing the activity of stress-related hormones like cortisol. Valeriana officinalis relieves stress, soothes insomnia, decreases inflammation, treats depression, prevents tremors, and regulates hormones by interacting with various neurotransmitter systems, including the GABA, serotonin, and dopamine systems.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is due to the presence of valerenic acid, valepotriates, and isovaltrate, which have been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative effects by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA and glutamate in the brain, resulting in a decrease in anxiety levels.
The sedative properties of this plant are attributed to its ability to interact with the GABA receptors in the brain, leading to a decrease in neuronal excitability and a promotion of relaxation.

The valerenic acid and valepotriates present in this plant have been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help improve sleep quality by reducing the time it takes to fall asleep and increasing the duration of sleep.
The anxiolytic and sedative effects of this plant can help enhance calmness by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, leading to a sense of calmness and well-being.
The stress-relieving properties of this plant are attributed to its ability to interact with the GABA receptors in the brain, leading to a decrease in stress levels and an improvement in mood.
The sedative properties of this plant can help soothe insomnia by reducing the time it takes to fall asleep and increasing the duration of sleep, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.

The anti-inflammatory properties of this plant are attributed to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, leading to a decrease in inflammation and an improvement in symptoms associated with inflammatory conditions.

The anxiolytic and sedative effects of this plant can help treat depression by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, leading to an improvement in mood and a reduction in symptoms associated with depression.

The sedative properties of this plant can help prevent tremors by reducing neuronal excitability and promoting relaxation, leading to a decrease in muscle tremors and an improvement in motor function.

The adaptogenic properties of this plant can help regulate hormones by reducing the impact of stress on the endocrine system, leading to an improvement in hormone balance and a reduction in symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Valeriana officinalis decreases blood pressure by exerting a vasodilatory effect, thereby relaxing blood vessel walls and reducing vascular resistance. It regulates heart rate by modulating the autonomic nervous system, which helps to stabilize heart rhythm and maintain a consistent pulse. Improved blood flow is facilitated by Valeriana officinalis, as it dilates blood vessels and increases the diameter of the vascular lumen. Valeriana officinalis stabilizes blood vessels by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which helps to prevent damage to the endothelium and maintain vascular health. The medicinal properties of Valeriana officinalis, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory properties, enhance cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation levels, increasing oxygen delivery, protecting against atherosclerosis, supporting healthy cholesterol, and improving circulation efficiency.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Valeriana officinalis soothes digestion by exerting anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Its antioxidant properties calm cramping by inhibiting the contraction of smooth muscle cells, while also relaxing muscles that contribute to gastrointestinal spasms. Valeriana officinalis improves motility through its ability to modulate gut motility, alleviates nausea due to its antispasmodic effects on the stomach, and prevents diarrhea by reducing the secretion of intestinal fluids. The plant's antimicrobial properties fight infections caused by pathogens in the digestive tract, while its cytoprotective effects heal ulcers by shielding the mucous membranes from oxidative damage and inflammation. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis eases bloating by reducing gas production in the gut, making it an effective natural remedy for various gastrointestinal issues.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Valeriana officinalis regulates hormonal imbalances by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, thanks to its adaptogenic properties that enable the body to respond to stress. This herb reduces cortisol levels by exerting an inhibitory effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, which is mediated by its anti-glucocorticoid activity. Valeriana officinalis supports adrenal function by stimulating the production of cortisol and other hormones, an effect attributed to its antioxidant properties that protect the adrenal glands from oxidative damage. The plant improves insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism through its anti-inflammatory action, which reduces inflammation in the pancreatic islets. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis stimulates thyroid function, decreases anxiety, regenerates ovaries, reduces hot flashes, improves menstrual irregularities, and balances estrogen levels by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis and exerting a mild estrogenic effect.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Valeriana officinalis soothes skin by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help calm irritation and reduce inflammation in the integumentary system. This plant also protects the skin barrier through its antimicrobial and astringent properties, preventing the entry of foreign pathogens and maintaining skin health. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis has wound-healing properties that aid in the regeneration of cells, promoting the healing of wounds and fighting infections caused by bacteria and fungi. Its antioxidant properties also stabilize skin by preventing oxidative stress, removing toxins that can harm the skin and promote cellular regeneration. Furthermore, the anti-inflammatory properties of Valeriana officinalis reduce inflammation, allowing the skin to regenerate cells and recover from damage, while its antimicrobial properties fight infections and protect the skin barrier.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Valeriana officinalis boosts lymphatic flow by activating the lymphatic vessels, improving the circulation of lymph fluid and reducing the accumulation of toxins. It reduces swelling quickly due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes that cause swelling. The plant's antioxidant properties also improve lymph node function by protecting them from oxidative damage, thereby enhancing their ability to filter and clean the lymph fluid. Valeriana officinalis enhances detoxification process by stimulating the lymphatic system to remove toxins and waste products, which helps to prevent the buildup of toxins in the body and reduces the risk of diseases. It stimulates lymphatic drainage, promotes lymphatic circulation, reduces inflammation faster, increases lymphocyte production, supports immune system function, and accelerates the wound healing process by regulating the lymphatic system's response to injury and inflammation.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Valeriana officinalis relaxes muscles through its flavonoid and phenolic compounds, which exert a sedative effect on muscle tissue. Reducing muscle spasm is also a significant health benefit, attributed to the anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties found in Valeriana officinalis. Decreasing joint pain is facilitated by the antioxidant activity of the plant, which neutralizes free radicals and reduces oxidative stress. Improving muscle tone is a result of the stimulatory effect of Valeriana officinalis on muscle contraction, mediated by its vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, this plant alleviates back pain, soothes muscle tension, stimulates bone growth, relieves joint inflammation, calms muscle fibrosis, and heals muscle damage through its unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which interact with the body's physiological processes to produce these beneficial effects.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Valeriana officinalis calms anxiety by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system due to its GABA receptor agonistic properties, which increase the activity of neurotransmitters responsible for promoting relaxation. This plant reduces stress through its adaptogenic properties, allowing the body to adapt to changing conditions and promoting balance in the nervous system. By regulating sleep, Valeriana officinalis helps to normalize the sleep-wake cycle, which is attributed to its ability to decrease the activity of neurons that promote wakefulness. Its ability to improve focus is a result of its effects on neurotransmitters such as dopamine and serotonin, which play a crucial role in attention and concentration. Valeriana officinalis also relaxes muscles, eases insomnia, soothes nerves, boosts mood, enhances relaxation, and reduces pain due to its anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and anxiolytic properties, which work synergistically to provide relief from various symptoms.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Valeriana officinalis regulates hormones by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in steroidogenesis, thus influencing the production of sex hormones. Improving fertility in both men and women is attributed to its antioxidant properties, which protect sperm and egg cells from oxidative damage. Enhancing libido is a result of its vasodilatory effects, which increase blood flow to the reproductive organs, promoting arousal. Valeriana officinalis reduces anxiety by inhibiting the activity of GABA receptors, a neurotransmitter involved in regulating stress and emotional responses, thereby inducing a calming effect. Additionally, it relieves depression and improves sleep by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters, including serotonin and dopamine, and enhances libido and increases energy by stimulating the release of these neurotransmitters, supports menstruation by regulating the uterine contractions, and reduces hot flashes by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in the synthesis of estrogen.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Valeriana officinalis relaxes airways through its vasodilatory properties, allowing for easier breathing. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties in this plant reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it a natural remedy for conditions like bronchitis and asthma. Valeriana officinalis fights infections with its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogens that can cause respiratory infections. Its cooling effect cools down the body and helps to soothe irritated airways, while opening passages in the respiratory tract to improve airflow. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis soothes coughs, eases congestion, prevents asthma attacks, and heals bronchitis by suppressing the production of mucus and other inflammatory mediators.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Valeriana officinalis relieves bladder issues by reducing muscle spasms in the bladder wall due to its antispasmodic properties. The antimicrobial properties in Valeriana officinalis prevent infections in the urinary system by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria. Valeriana officinalis reduces inflammation in the urinary tract by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, thereby alleviating symptoms of cystitis. Improving urination is facilitated by the diuretic properties of Valeriana officinalis, which increases urine production and reduces the risk of urinary retention. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis eases pain, soothes irritation, boosts antioxidants, slows prostate enlargement, cures nocturnal enuresis, and calms the nervous system due to its sedative and antispasmodic properties, making it a valuable herb for maintaining urinary health.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Valeriana officinalis are multifaceted, with its antimicrobial properties attributed to the presence of valepotriates, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of various bacteria and fungi, making it effective in treating infections. Its antioxidant properties, mediated by flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, contributing to its potential in mitigating neurodegenerative diseases. Valeriana officinalis also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, due in part to the presence of isovaltrate and valerenic acid, which have been found to suppress the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. The plant's anxiolytic and sedative effects are largely attributed to the presence of valepotriates and valerenic acid, which interact with the GABA receptor, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. Additionally, its analgesic, antispasmodic, antihistamine, and anticonvulsant properties have been linked to the presence of valepotriates, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic potential in treating a range of conditions.

properties_list.description: Valeriana officinalis has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, particularly against certain bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of compounds such as valerenic acid and isovalerenic acid, which have been found to inhibit the growth of microorganisms.

Valeriana officinalis contains antioxidants like valerenic acid and isovalerenic acid, which have been found to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, potentially protecting against cell damage and inflammation.

Valeriana officinalis has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and isovalerenic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in various studies.

Valeriana officinalis has been traditionally used as an anxiolytic agent, and studies have supported its efficacy in reducing anxiety in individuals, likely due to the presence of valerenic acid and other compounds that interact with the GABA receptor and modulate the activity of the central nervous system.

Valeriana officinalis has been found to possess sedative properties, attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and other compounds that interact with the GABA receptor and modulate the activity of the central nervous system, leading to a calming effect and improved sleep quality.

Valeriana officinalis has been traditionally used as an analgesic agent, and studies have supported its efficacy in reducing pain in individuals, likely due to the presence of valerenic acid and other compounds that interact with opioid receptors and modulate the activity of the central nervous system.

Valeriana officinalis has been found to possess antispasmodic properties, attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and other compounds that relax smooth muscle tissue and alleviate cramping and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract and other areas of the body.

There is limited evidence to support the antihistamine properties of Valeriana officinalis, and more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness in alleviating allergic reactions and histamine-related symptoms.

Valeriana officinalis has been traditionally used as an anticonvulsant agent, and some studies have supported its efficacy in reducing seizure frequency and severity in individuals with epilepsy, likely due to the presence of valerenic acid and other compounds that modulate the activity of the central nervous system.

constituents.description: Valerenic acid is an isovaleryl ester of isovaleric acid, a gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) receptor antagonist and a modulator of the GABA_A receptor.
Valeranone is a sesquiterpene lactone with sedative and anxiolytic properties.
Phenolic acids are a type of bioactive compound found in Valeriana officinalis, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

Saponins found in Valeriana officinalis are known for their foaming properties, bitterness, and astringency, with a chemical structure consisting of a triterpene or steroid backbone and a sugar moiety.
A type of plant compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Valerenal is a sesquiterpene alkaloid found in Valeriana officinalis, exhibiting sedative, anxiolytic, and hypnotic properties.
Triterpenoids are found in Valeriana officinalis and possess sedative, anxiolytic, and spasmolytic properties.
Coumarins, found in Valeriana officinalis, are known for their antispasmodic, sedative, and anxiolytic properties.
Isovaleric acid is a short-chain fatty acid with sedative and hypnotic properties, known to induce relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Valerian root alkaloids, such as valerenic acid and valerenol, exhibit sedative, anxiolytic, and antispasmodic properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Valeriana officinalis are valerenic acid, valeranone, phenolic acids, saponins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Valerenic acid and valeranone have been shown to have sedative and anxiolytic effects, whereas phenolic acids possess antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress. Saponins in Valeriana officinalis have been found to have adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory effects, while flavonoids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, highlighting their potential in the treatment of various health disorders. The combination of these bioactive compounds is thought to be responsible for the herb's ability to regulate sleep patterns, alleviate anxiety and stress, and exhibit antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties. The medicinal constituents of Valeriana officinalis have been extensively studied, and their mechanisms of action have been attributed to their ability to interact with neurotransmitters and modulate the activity of various enzymes.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Valeriana officinalis for medicinal purposes are roots, rhizomes, and flowers. The roots contain valepotriates, valerenic acid, and valeoptenate, which have sedative, anxiolytic, and analgesic properties due to their ability to modulate GABA receptors and inhibit neuronal excitability. The rhizomes contain valerenic acid and valepotriates, which exhibit anxiolytic and sedative effects through their action on the GABA receptor and inhibition of the monoamine oxidase enzyme. The flowers contain valerenic acid, valepotriates, and valeranone, which possess sedative, anxiolytic, and anti-inflammatory properties due to their ability to inhibit the lipoxygenase enzyme and modulate the GABA receptor. These bioactive compounds contribute to the roots', rhizomes', and flowers' primary medicinal properties, which include sedation, anxiolysis, analgesia, and anti-inflammatory effects.

parts_list.description: The roots of this plant are rich in valerenic acid, valeranone, and valepotriates, exhibiting sedative, anxiolytic, and antispasmodic properties, which provide relief from insomnia, anxiety, and muscle spasms, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.

The rhizomes of this plant contain valepotriates, valerenic acid, and isovaleric acid, displaying sedative, anticonvulsant, and antispasmodic effects, which help alleviate epilepsy, anxiety disorders, and muscle spasms, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities.

The flowers of this plant are rich in valerenic acid and valepotriates, demonstrating sedative, anxiolytic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce stress, anxiety, and inflammation, promoting relaxation and improving mood.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, restlessness, and other sleep disorders, as well as to relieve muscle tension and promote relaxation.
preparations.overview: Valeriana officinalis tincture is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat various health conditions including insomnia, anxiety, muscle spasms, restlessness, sleep disorders, stress, digestive issues, hypertension, nervousness and menopause symptoms. It's one of the most widely used herbal remedies due to its effectiveness. The common effect of this preparation is sedation, making it a popular choice for people with trouble sleeping. The tincture is made from the roots and rhizomes of the Valeriana officinalis plant, which are rich in valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, the active compounds responsible for its therapeutic properties. The process of making Valeriana officinalis tincture is moderately difficult, requiring precise measurements and careful preparation of the plant material, but the end result is a highly potent and versatile herbal remedy.
recipe: Gather 1 part Valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes, and 2 parts solvent (such as vodka or ethanol) in a clean glass jar.
Combine the Valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes with the solvent in the jar, making sure the roots and rhizomes are completely covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, allowing it to steep for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass bottles and store it in a cool, dark place, using 20-30 drops (about 1-2 ml) per dose as needed.

preparations.description: Essential oil made from this plant is used to reduce anxiety, promote sleep, and alleviate muscle and joint pain, as well as to treat restlessness and insomnia.
preparations.overview: Valeriana officinalis essential oil is commonly used to treat various health issues, including insomnia, anxiety, restlessness, stress, digestive issues, muscle spasms, sleep disorders, and nervousness. It's widely used due to its popularity and effectiveness. The common effect of this oil is sedative, which helps in reducing muscle spasms, anxiety, and restlessness. This oil is primarily made from the roots and rhizomes of the Valeriana officinalis plant. However, producing high-quality essential oil from these plant parts can be challenging due to their complex composition and the need for precise distillation techniques.
recipe: Harvest Valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes at the peak of their potency, ideally during the root harvest season.
Dry the roots and rhizomes thoroughly to a moisture content of 10%, either by air-drying or using a dehydrator.
Use a solvent extraction method, such as hydrodistillation, to extract the essential oil from the dried roots and rhizomes.
Filter the extracted essential oil to remove any impurities and sediment, resulting in a clear, pale-yellow liquid.
Use a dosage of 1-3% Valeriana officinalis essential oil in skincare or aromatherapy products, diluted with a carrier oil or other ingredients.

preparations.description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, restlessness, and other sleep disorders, as well as to reduce stress and muscle spasms.
preparations.overview: Valeriana officinalis capsule is widely used to treat insomnia, anxiety, muscle spasms, stress, restlessness, digestive issues, hypertension, restless leg syndrome, menopause symptoms and depression. It's a very common herbal remedy, often found in health food stores and online. The common effect of Valeriana officinalis capsule is sedation, which is usually observed within 30 minutes to an hour after consumption. This preparation is typically made from the roots and rhizomes of the Valeriana officinalis plant. The process of making Valeriana officinalis capsule involves moderately difficult steps, including extraction and encapsulation of the plant material.
recipe: Harvest and dry Valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a suitable capsule filler material in a ratio of 1:3 (herbal powder: filler material) for easy encapsulation.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the herbal powder mixture, making sure to follow the standard dosage of 500-1000 mg per capsule.
Store the Valeriana officinalis herbal capsules in a cool, dry place and consume 1-2 capsules 2-3 times a day as needed.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is applied topically to relieve anxiety, insomnia, restlessness, and minor muscle and joint pain.
preparations.overview: Valeriana officinalis salve is a topical preparation used to treat various conditions, including insomnia, anxiety, muscle spasm, stress, restless leg syndrome, restlessness, rheumatism, headaches, and nerve pain. It's a common herbal remedy, widely used due to its effectiveness in alleviating these issues. The salve typically has a sedative effect, helping to calm the mind and body. Valeriana officinalis salve is made from the roots and rhizomes of the valerian plant, which contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds responsible for its therapeutic properties. The process of making this salve can be moderately difficult, requiring some experience with herbalism and the proper preparation of the plant material, but the end result is a natural and effective treatment for various health issues.
recipe: Combine 1 part Valeriana officinalis roots and 1 part rhizomes in a clean glass jar, covering the contents with 80-proof vodka to create a 1:1 root to vodka ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every 2-3 days to ensure the roots and rhizomes release their active compounds into the vodka.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 1 part of the reserved liquid with 1 part of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 180°F to 190°F.
Pour the liquid mixture into small tin containers or a glass jar, allowing it to cool and solidify before labeling and storing in a cool, dark place.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat insomnia, restlessness, anxiety, and nervousness due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties.
preparations.overview: Valeriana officinalis tea is a herbal remedy used to treat a range of health issues including insomnia, anxiety, stress, muscle spasms, restlessness, digestive issues, hypertension, sleep disorders and nervousness. This preparation is widely used due to its effectiveness in addressing various health concerns. The common effect of consuming Valeriana officinalis tea is sedation and relaxation. It's made from the roots and rhizomes of the Valeriana officinalis plant, which are rich in valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds. The preparation is relatively easy to make, as dried roots and rhizomes can be steeped in hot water to create a soothing tea.
recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried Valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the dried roots and rhizomes.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the roots and rhizomes to release their active compounds.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids, reserving the herbal tea for consumption.
Consume 1/2 cup of the herbal tea, 2-3 times a day, to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.

preparations.description: Suppository made from this plant is a medicinal preparation used to treat restlessness, nervousness, and insomnia due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties.
preparations.overview: Valeriana officinalis suppository is a herbal preparation used to treat various conditions, including insomnia, anxiety, digestive issues, restless leg syndrome, restlessness, muscle spasms, sleep disorders, menopausal hot flashes, menopause, and depression. This preparation is less commonly used due to its limited application. Its effects are also not well-documented, making it a less conventional choice for treatment. Valeriana officinalis suppositories are made from roots and rhizomes of the valerian plant. The manufacturing process of this suppository is complex, requiring precise extraction of valerenic acid and valepotriates from the plant's roots and rhizomes.
recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes, and 1 teaspoon of natural suppository base.
Grind the dried Valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground Valeriana officinalis powder with the suppository base until well combined.
Fill suppository molds with the Valeriana officinalis mixture, and refrigerate until set.
Dosage: 1 suppository, inserted as needed, up to 3 times daily, under the guidance of a healthcare practitioner.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Valeriana officinalis for medicinal purposes are tinctures, used to treat anxiety and insomnia due to their valerenic acid content, which modulates the GABA receptor. Essential oil, obtained through steam distillation, is used topically for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, often used in aromatherapy for its sedative effects. Capsules are used to deliver a standardized dose of valerian root extract, providing relief from restlessness and insomnia. A valerian salve is applied topically to relieve muscle and joint pain, as well as to aid in sleep. Valerian tea is consumed for its sedative properties, and suppositories are used to treat restlessness and insomnia in cases where oral consumption is not feasible.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Valeriana officinalis interacts with the sedative benzodiazepines, increasing the risk of respiratory depression and sedation.
Valerian root may exacerbate liver disease
Valeriana officinalis induces sedation through the GABA-A receptor activation.
Valeriana officinalis exacerbates symptoms of pre-existing respiratory conditions such as asthma.
Valerian root extracts induce sedation and promote sleep, often leading to a state of reduced consciousness or trance-like condition.

Valeriana officinalis has been shown to cause memory loss in high doses due to its sedative properties, including a case report of a patient experiencing amnesia after consuming large amounts of valerian root extract.
Valerian root extract causes a sudden loss of balance and equilibrium due to decreased blood pressure and vasodilation.
Valeriana officinalis contains valerenic acid and valepotriates, which act as sedatives to induce sleepiness and drowsiness.
High doses of Valeriana officinalis essential oil cause gastrointestinal irritation leading to nausea
Vomiting occurs in individuals consuming large doses of Valeriana officinalis, causing gastrointestinal irritation.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Valeriana officinalis are interactions with medications such as sedatives, blood thinners, and certain antidepressants, which can interact with the plant. In some cases, the use of Valeriana officinalis can aggravate pre-existing conditions like liver disease, heart problems, or high blood pressure. The plant may also induce seizures in rare cases, especially in individuals with a history of seizure disorders. Furthermore, improper use can exacerbate anxiety and panic disorders, which can lead to an increase in symptoms. The sedative properties of the plant can hypnotize users, causing them to fall into a deep sleep, while amnesia and dizziness can occur due to the plant's ability to affect cognitive function, leading to drowsiness and an increased risk of nausea and vomiting.

precautions.precaution_description: Pregnant or breastfeeding women should consult a healthcare professional due to lack of data on valerian root's safety.
Valeriana officinalis may stimulate uterine contractions, potentially affecting pregnancy, so it's recommended to use under medical supervision if pregnant or breastfeeding.
Valeriana officinalis supports liver enzyme production and function.
Consume Valeriana officinalis in moderation due to potential blood pressure lowering effects.
Concurrent use with sedatives increases risk of excessive sedation and respiratory depression.
Concurrent use with monoamine oxidase inhibitors increases risk of adverse interactions.
Concurrent use with anticoagulants increases risk of bleeding.
Valerian root interacts with diuretic medications, increasing risk of potassium depletion.
Concurrent use with antihypertensive agents increases risk of hypotension.
Concurrent use with anesthetics can cause potentiation of sedative effects, leading to increased respiratory and cardiac depression.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Valeriana officinalis medicinally are consulting a healthcare professional, especially for individuals with pre-existing conditions, as it may interact with certain medications. Determining pregnancy status is crucial, as valerian root may stimulate uterine contractions and affect fetal development. Establishing liver function is also essential, as valerian root can be hepatotoxic in rare cases and may exacerbate existing liver conditions. Additionally, monitoring blood pressure is necessary, as valerian root can cause dizziness and orthostatic hypotension in some individuals. Other concurrent medications to avoid include sedatives, MAOIs, anticoagulants, diuretics, antihypertensives, and anesthetics, which may enhance valerian root's sedative effects or interact with its mechanisms.
identification: Valeriana officinalis is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Valerianaceae family, characterized by a woody rhizome, hairy stems and leaves, and small, white or pale pink, fragrant flowers in a raceme or panicle. It typically grows to 1-2 meters in height, with pinnate leaves composed of 3-5 pairs of leaflets, each 2-5 cm long, and a strong, sweet, and earthy scent. Native to Europe, Asia, and parts of North America, it has been widely naturalized in many regions, and can be found growing in moist, temperate climates, often in woodland edges, along streams, and in shady areas. This plant thrives in rich, well-drained soils, and can tolerate a range of conditions, from full sun to partial shade, with moderate watering requirements. Its underground rhizome and roots allow it to survive harsh winters and dry periods, making it a resilient and adaptable species.
cultivation: Valeriana officinalis is a perennial plant that thrives in moist, temperate climates with mild winters and cool summers, typically requiring a minimum of 1,000 hours of chill hours to induce dormancy. It grows best in well-drained soil with a pH between 5.5 and 7.0, often found in woodland edges, riverbanks, and mountain meadows. To cultivate this plant, it's essential to provide it with partial shade, especially in warmer climates, and maintain consistent moisture levels, although it can tolerate some drought. Growth tips include dividing and replanting established roots in the spring, and mulching around the base to retain moisture and suppress weeds, which can lead to a bountiful harvest of roots and leaves. Regular pruning of the foliage and flowers can also promote bushy growth and encourage the plant to focus its energy on root development.
history: Valeriana officinalis is a plant that has been used historically as a medicine for its sedative and calming properties, dating back to ancient Greece and Rome where it was used to treat insomnia, restlessness, and anxiety. The root of the plant, also known as valerian root, was used in traditional medicine to relieve symptoms of sleep disorders, muscle cramps, and spasms. In the 19th century, valerian root was used as a substitute for opium in some medical applications, due to its sedative properties. Valeriana officinalis was also used to treat nervous disorders, such as epilepsy and hysteria, and as a treatment for menstrual cramps and other gynecological issues. The plant's medicinal uses were extensively documented in the European pharmacopeia, where it was valued for its ability to induce relaxation and reduce anxiety.
